Brasse-Bouillon

How it works The app FAQ Join the waitlist
2026 beta · Limited spots

Brew your first beer without fear of messing it up.

From the recipe you pick to your first sip, the app tells you what to do, when, and why. No jargon, no mental math, no nasty surprises.

Join the waitlist See how it goes

Heads-up for English speakers: the app’s interface launches in French first -
an English version is on the way. This site is already in English so you can follow along today.

The journey

From recipe to first pour

Four steps, a copilot at every moment. You’re never left alone in front of the kettle.

Screenshots throughout this page show the app’s French interface - the English app UI is in the works.

  1. 01

    Pick your recipe

    Compare the community’s clone recipes - rated and battle-tested - and adopt the one that tempts you. No jargon.

  2. 02

    Brew step by step

    Timers, temperatures and the why behind every move: the app guides you through mash, boil and chill.

  3. 03

    Track fermentation

    Days, gravity, temperature. One glance and you know where you stand.

  4. 04

    Bottle it, savor it

    Slap on your label, share with friends, and enjoy your first creation.

Mash· Hopping· IBU 22· ABV 4.8 %· EBC 8· Fermentation· Cold crash· Bottle conditioning· Bottling· Mash· Hopping· IBU 22· ABV 4.8 %· EBC 8· Fermentation· Cold crash· Bottle conditioning· Bottling·

Three tools, one copilot

The essentials, and nothing but

No feature bloat. Three pillars, built to save you time and help you level up.

Recipe studio

Create, clone and tweak your recipes in a few taps. Version and compare without losing the thread.

Brewing calculators

IBU, ABV, color, volumes, efficiency. Every number in one place, and reliable.

Guided brew control

A clear timeline, useful reminders, critical checkpoints. You know what to do, when, and why.

Recognition

Loved a beer? Scan it.

Scan the label of a store-bought beer : Brasse-Bouillon identifies it, then suggests equivalent recipes rated by the community - ready to brew.

Instant recognition

Style, color, bitterness, ABV : the full profile the moment the beer is identified.

Community recipes

Clones rated and proven by other brewers - import them into your notebook in one tap.

Learn as you brew

Understand what you brew

Not just how, but why. The Academy gathers sourced articles and a brewing glossary - from ingredients to fermentation, from your first batch to fine-tuning.

Landmarks, not jargon

Ingredients, color, alcohol, fermentation: clear articles, sorted by theme, to learn at your own pace.

A sourced glossary

IBU, gravity, attenuation… every term defined simply, with sources - brewing books and standards.

Frequently asked

The doubts before you dive in

Do I need to be handy to brew my own beer?

Not at all. A pot, a fermenter, some bottles - and the app telling you what to do at every step. If you can cook pasta, you can brew a beer.

How much does it cost to get started?

Roughly €60-120 for a complete starter kit. You’ll reuse it for 5-6 batches, and the gear pays for itself fast compared with buying good craft beer.

How long does a batch take (the first time)?

Half a day for the brew session (4-5h), then 2 to 3 weeks of fermentation where you’ve got almost nothing to do. The app reminds you of the critical checkpoints.

And if I botch my first batch?

It happens, and it’s almost always drinkable. The app walks back through each step to help you understand what happened - and your next batch comes out better.

When will the app be available?

A rolling beta through 2026. Waitlist signups get first access.

Get involved

Join the first batch

Drop your email to get into the beta loop. You can also share your take or write to us directly.

By subscribing, you agree to our terms, our privacy policy and our cookie policy.

3 to 5 minutes to help us build the right app. Your answers directly steer what we develop. Everything is anonymous; contact details are only asked at the end, and they’re optional.

How long have you been brewing?
Roughly how many batches a year?
Where do you brew?
Where do your recipes and brew notes live today? (multiple answers)
Which tool(s) or app do you use? (multiple answers)
Do you ever botch a batch, or get lost during the process?
Have you ever tried to reproduce (clone) a specific commercial beer?
Do you share your recipes with other brewers?
If you share a recipe, does being credited as the author matter to you?
What would make you try a new brewing app? (multiple answers)
Would you be up for a 15-min chat to tell me more?

See our privacy policy and our terms.

Enjoying the project? You can also support its development by buying me a beer.

B·B

Brasse-Bouillon, the app for curious homebrewers. Get in touch via the site form.

Want to support the project? Buy me a beer.

Alcohol abuse is dangerous for your health. Drink responsibly. Brasse-Bouillon is for adults and promotes responsible consumption.
